task_completion.description: Log in to the OpenMRS Administration interface
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenMRS Administration dashboard is visible, indicating successful login with the provided admin credentials.

task_completion.description: Navigate to the user creation screen
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Add User', 'Manage Users', or 'Create User' interface is open in the web browser.
The person details form shows 'Anita' in the Given Name field, 'Sharma' in the Family Name field, and Gender is selected as 'Female' or 'F'.
The user details form shows the username field filled with 'anita.sharma' and the password fields populated.

task_completion.description: Assign the 'Provider' role to the new user
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Provider' role is checked or highlighted in the roles selection area of the user creation form.

task_completion.description: Successfully save the new user account
task_completion.visual_evidence: A success message (e.g., 'User Saved') is displayed, or the newly created user 'anita.sharma' is visible in the system's user list.

integrity.description: The agent used a web browser to interact with the application
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a web browser (like Chrome or Firefox) navigating to localhost/openmrs/admin, rather than terminal commands (like curl) being used to send API requests.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual OpenMRS GUI
integrity.visual_evidence: The visual elements correspond to the genuine OpenMRS web interface, not a text editor, script, or database client.

integrity.description: The agent manually entered the data into the UI forms
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the forms being actively filled out in the browser, rather than a script executing in the background to bypass the UI.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The user must be created with exact demographic and account details: Given Name 'Anita', Family Name 'Sharma', Gender 'F' (Female), Username 'anita.sharma', and Role 'Provider'. The task must be completed within the OpenMRS Administration interface (https://localhost/openmrs/admin).
